content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed three amendments against the milestone export-beads flow. All three identify legitimate concerns: (1) adapter_id keyed only by milestone_id lets a new invocation treat a prior run's pending mutation journal as its own and flush stale work — real shared-state/isolation gap; (2) export idempotency is keyed on normalized title only, so bundles with distinct explicit bead_ids but shared titles fail re-export with multiple-active-matches — confirmed in `summary_matches_materialized_bead`; (3) `materialize_bead_entry` early-returns on reuse without refreshing title/description/priority/labels, so plan edits silently fail to propagate on re-export — genuine correctness/maintainability gap. All three accepted.

## Votes

- `fr-4-66faaedf`: Accept
  The adapter_id in src/cli/milestone.rs:373 is `milestone-export-beads-<milestone_id>` — deterministic per milestone. `ensure_current_adapter_owns_pending_snapshot` (br_process.rs:1181-1217) treats any record with a matching adapter_id as 'owned' and lets `sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy` flush it during preflight. A crashed or concurrent prior invocation against the same milestone (with a since-edited bundle) will therefore have its pending `create_bead` mutations published into `.beads` as if they belonged to the current run. Per the review rules, shared mutable state keyed only on milestone id is a real defect even if current callers haven't hit it, and including a bundle/plan hash plus per-attempt ownership token is a sound remediation.
- `fr-4-7a12aec2`: Accept
  Confirmed: `existing_by_title` (service.rs:1530-1541) and `summary_matches_materialized_bead` (service.rs:1895-1908) key purely on normalized title + bead type + labels, never on the canonical proposal id. `canonical_export_proposal_id` is computed and inserted into `proposal_id_map` (service.rs:1666) but only used for resolving `depends_on` references. Meanwhile `bundle.rs:221-234` only dedupes non-explicit titles, so a valid bundle may legitimately contain two beads with distinct explicit `bead_id`s but the same title. On re-export both proposals collide in `existing_by_title`, triggering the `active_eligible_indices.len() > 1` error at service.rs:1965-1973. Using a persisted proposal-id → actual-bead-id map as the durable idempotency key fixes this correctly.
- `fr-4-447a5b66`: Accept
  Verified: `materialize_bead_entry` (service.rs:1743-1776) calls `find_existing_materialized_bead` and early-returns the existing `MaterializedBeadState` when one is found, performing no diff against the current `MaterializeBeadInput`. Only `ensure_dependency` and `ensure_bead_comment` repair structure, and even they only add missing items — they never reconcile divergence. Re-running `milestone export-beads` after editing a plan (rewritten description, shifted priority, changed labels) therefore silently leaves the stored bead stale, which is a real correctness gap for the export-beads contract. Option (b) — emit a warning/journal entry on drift — is a low-risk minimum that preserves the user's trust in re-export and matches the existing 'repair missing structure' ethos without overwriting human edits.
